methods.description: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) was conducted to discriminate bee pollen samples based on their botanical origin using physicochemical, organic acids, sugars, TPC, TFC, phenolics compounds, amino acids, and fatty acids data. The analysis involved examining the variance explained by the principal components, interpreting variable loadings, and analyzing sample scores for clustering.

steps.description: The PCA was conducted based on the analysis discussed in order to discriminate the bee pollen samples based on their botanical origin.
steps.searchable_summary: perform principal component analysis on multivariate dataset

steps.description: The first two principal components (PC1 and PC2) explained 77% of the data variance (PC1 explained 50% of the data variance, while PC2 explained 27% of the data variance).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and report variance explained by principal components
outputs: Percentage of variance explained by each principal component

steps.description: The PC1 is influenced strongly by lipids and negatively by raffinose and C18:2, the PC2 is influenced positively by quercetin while myricetin influences it negatively. Propionic acid, C17:1, TFC and asparagine do not influence the projection of the scores due to their closeness to the origins of the two PC.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret PCA loadings to identify influential variables for each component
outputs: Identification of variables strongly influencing PC1 and PC2

steps.description: As can be seen in Figure 2A, the samples with high percentage of Helianthus annus, Robinia pseudoacacia, Pinaceae spp., Quercus spp. and Prunus spp. formed clusters which confirms that the analysis carried out is useful for their discrimination. Regarding the polyfloral bee pollen it can be observed that the samples are near on the other one but they include in their region the bee pollen from Crataegus monogyna, Tilia spp. and Taraxacum.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret PCA scores for sample clustering based on botanical origin and visualize scores plot

steps.description: The Quercus spp. and Crataegus monogyna exhibited a high myricetin content, while Prunus spp. exhibited a high quercetin content. The Helianthus annus samples exhibited a high free acidity, C18:0, F/G, lactic acid, lipids and C20:1. The polyfloral samples exhibited a high by raffinose and C18:2, myricetin and raffinose ( Figure 2B). The Robinia pseudoacacia pollen samples were associated with C15:0, chlorogenic acid, turanose, maltose, C17:0, and p-hydroxibenzoic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze variable associations with sample groups using PCA loadings and scores plots
outputs: Identification of variables characteristic of specific sample groups or botanical origins

steps.description: A high positive correlation between moisture content and water activity (r = 0.898), moisture content and F/G (r = 0.818) was observed.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pairwise correlation coefficients between selected variables
